description: Hardened psychology knowledge index for Marion ingestion. Normalizes affect interpretation, attachment patterns, cognitive distortions, crisis flags, support strategies, and trauma sensitivity into a retrieval-ready structure with formatter, suppression, and continuity guidance.

affect_interpretation.description: Maps emotional language to likely affective states and support needs.

attachment_patterns.description: Maps relational language to attachment-related interpretations.

cognitive_distortions.description: Maps rigid or distorted thinking patterns to reframing support.

crisis_flags.description: Identifies crisis or safety-oriented language requiring escalation.

support_strategies.description: Defines preferred response structures and support patterns.

trauma_sensitivity.description: Adds pacing, consent, safety, and regulation guidance for trauma-linked material.

records.title: High-arousal overwhelm
records.summary: Language that suggests overload, spiraling, urgency, or inability to regulate in the moment.
records.interpretation: Possible acute overload with narrowed tolerance for complexity and reduced cognitive bandwidth.
responseGuidance: Offer one immediate next step only
contraindications: Do not jump to problem-solving too quickly

records.title: Low-activation shutdown
records.summary: Language that suggests numbness, flatness, depletion, collapse, or reduced initiative.
records.interpretation: Possible emotional blunting or depleted activation with limited readiness for pressure-heavy engagement.
contraindications: Do not apply aggressive motivational pressure

records.title: Self-evaluative shame pattern
records.summary: Language focused on defectiveness, worthlessness, inadequacy, or harsh self-judgment.
records.interpretation: Possible shame-dominant framing where identity is being collapsed into a painful state or outcome.
contraindications: Do not use blunt challenge too early

records.title: Recovery and positive shift
records.summary: Language that suggests relief, stabilization, renewed agency, or emotional improvement.
signals: i think i can do this
records.interpretation: Possible active recovery or improved regulation with readiness for forward motion.
contraindications: Do not pull user backward into distress framing

strongSignals: i think i can do this

records.title: Anxious attachment activation
records.summary: Fear of disconnection, reassurance-seeking, or intense preoccupation with relational security.
signals: i need to know they still care
i keep checking for a reply
i can't stop thinking about what they meant
records.interpretation: Possible attachment activation organized around uncertainty, closeness threat, and reassurance-seeking.
responseGuidance: Validate attachment alarm without escalating it
contraindications: Do not prematurely declare the other person unsafe

strongSignals: i need to know they still care
i keep checking for a reply
i can't stop thinking about what they meant

records.title: Avoidant withdrawal pattern
records.summary: Distancing, emotional minimization, or protective self-sufficiency under relational strain.
signals: i don't want to need anyone
i shut down when people get too close
records.interpretation: Possible protective deactivation pattern with distance used to preserve control or reduce vulnerability.
responseGuidance: Respect autonomy while naming the protective pattern gently
contraindications: Do not frame distance as moral failure
Do not push intimacy before safety

strongSignals: i don't want to need anyone
i shut down when people get too close

records.title: Push-pull relational conflict
records.summary: Alternation between closeness-seeking and fear/withdrawal, often with confusion and volatility.
signals: i want them close and then i want them gone
i don't trust it when it feels safe
i panic when they leave and panic when they stay
records.interpretation: Possible conflicting attachment impulses where closeness and threat feel fused.
responseGuidance: Focus on present regulation before relational analysis
contraindications: Do not oversimplify into one motive

strongSignals: i want them close and then i want them gone
i don't trust it when it feels safe
i panic when they leave and panic when they stay

records.title: Catastrophizing
records.summary: Projecting the worst possible outcome and treating it as likely or inevitable.
signals: everything is going to fall apart
it's going to be a disaster
there's no way this ends well
records.interpretation: Possible threat amplification with future-oriented worst-case projection.
responseGuidance: Shrink time horizon to the next actionable step

strongSignals: everything is going to fall apart
it's going to be a disaster
there's no way this ends well

records.title: All-or-nothing thinking
records.summary: Interpreting outcomes in rigid extremes with little room for nuance.
signals: if it's not perfect it's worthless
i either win or i fail
records.interpretation: Possible binary evaluation pattern that narrows flexibility and inflates failure meaning.
responseGuidance: Look for partial truth and mixed evidence

strongSignals: if it's not perfect it's worthless
i either win or i fail

records.title: Mind reading
records.summary: Assuming others' intentions, judgments, or emotional states without enough evidence.
signals: i know they meant it as a jab
records.interpretation: Possible projection of internal fear into assumed external judgment.
responseGuidance: Distinguish observed facts from inferred meaning
contraindications: Do not overvalidate the assumption as fact

strongSignals: i know they meant it as a jab

records.title: Overgeneralization
records.summary: Using one event or repeated pain point to define the entire future or self.
signals: nothing ever works out for me
records.interpretation: Possible globalizing pattern that turns limited evidence into broad inevitability.
responseGuidance: Look for exceptions without forcing positivity
contraindications: Do not answer with simplistic contradiction only

strongSignals: nothing ever works out for me

records.title: Self-harm or suicidal language
records.summary: Statements suggesting desire to die, self-harm, disappearance, or inability to stay safe.
signals: i don't want to be here
everyone would be better off without me
records.interpretation: Immediate safety concern requiring crisis-oriented handling.
responseGuidance: Encourage immediate human crisis support and emergency help where relevant

strongSignals: i don't want to be here
everyone would be better off without me

records.title: Imminent harm language
records.summary: Statements suggesting imminent violence, loss of control, or risk to others.
signals: i can't control what i'm about to do
someone is going to get hurt
records.interpretation: Possible imminent harm escalation requiring immediate de-escalation and safety response.
responseGuidance: Encourage stepping away and contacting emergency/human support
contraindications: Do not continue regular support mode

strongSignals: i can't control what i'm about to do
someone is going to get hurt

records.title: Severe panic or disorientation
records.summary: Language suggesting inability to orient, breathe, slow down, or remain grounded.
records.interpretation: Possible acute dysregulation requiring immediate grounding and low-complexity response.
responseGuidance: Focus on immediate safety and regulation

records.title: Stabilize, then narrow
records.summary: Use when the user is emotionally activated and cognitive bandwidth is low.

records.title: Reflect, then clarify
records.summary: Use when the user is emotionally coherent but uncertain, conflicted, or tangled in interpretation.

records.title: Affirm and channel
records.summary: Use when the user shows recovery, pride, relief, or constructive momentum.
responsePattern: turn momentum into one sustainable action
contraindications: Do not drag the user back into distress framing

records.title: Repair without shame
records.summary: Use when the user is fused with shame, guilt, or self-condemnation.
contraindications: Do not use hard challenge too early

records.title: Do not force traumatic detail
records.summary: When the user hints at painful or destabilizing experiences, preserve choice and pacing.
signals: i don't want to talk about all of it
i can't go back into that right now
records.interpretation: Possible trauma-linked material where pacing and agency matter more than detail extraction.
responseGuidance: Anchor in present safety and control

strongSignals: i don't want to talk about all of it
i can't go back into that right now

records.title: Trigger activation response
records.summary: Present-day cues may reactivate fear, shame, or physiological alarm linked to earlier experiences.
signals: i reacted way bigger than i expected
i know it's small but it hit hard
records.interpretation: Possible trigger-driven activation where intensity may exceed the present cue.
responseGuidance: Differentiate past activation from present reality gently
contraindications: Do not call the user irrational

strongSignals: i reacted way bigger than i expected
i know it's small but it hit hard

records.title: Use control-and-choice language
records.summary: Trauma-sensitive response often benefits from explicit restoration of agency, pacing, and consent in conversation.
signals: i can't do too much at once
i need this to feel safe
records.interpretation: Conversation itself may need to become more choice-oriented and less directive.
responseGuidance: Offer one choice at a time
Let the user set pacing where possible
contraindications: Do not push narrative depth for its own sake

strongSignals: i can't do too much at once
i need this to feel safe
